title: lac CLI
problem: Developers need a terminal interface for the entire feature provenance lifecycle — creating features, running quality checks, tracing ownership, and operating the local server — without leaving their workflow.
analysis: A Commander.js-based CLI exposes 11 commands covering the full lifecycle. Commands are thin wrappers over shared lib/ utilities so they are individually testable. The serve command bridges the CLI and LSP worlds by spawning lac-lsp and opening the browser. As of iteration 3, 23 Vitest tests cover loadConfig, doctor, lint, and blame commands.
implementation: Commands: workspace (init/status), init, spawn, tag, archive, lint (--watch), blame, export (--site), hooks (install/uninstall), serve, doctor, search. Built with tsdown to ESM. Installed globally as `lac` binary. Reads lac.config.json from workspace root for default options and paths.
validation: 23 Vitest tests cover: loadConfig parsing and defaults, doctor validation errors, lint threshold and required-fields checks, blame path resolution and edge cases.

decisions.rationale: Commander has the cleanest subcommand model and TypeScript support without runtime type gymnastics.

decisions.decision: lac serve spawns lac-lsp as a child process rather than embedding the HTTP server
decisions.rationale: Keeps packages independently deployable. lac-cli stays lightweight. lac-lsp can be used standalone.

knownLimitations: lint --watch uses fs.watch which is unreliable on Linux (use chokidar in a future iteration)
lac diff command not yet implemented
